description:
## The change

Replace the typer's "emit specs into a flat bag, prune at the end"
model with proper emitter→target provenance.

### Data model

\`\`\`rust
enum EmitSlot {
    Direct,                          // the Call/TailCall callee
    Cont,                            // the continuation of Call/CallClosure/Receive
    ClosureLit(usize, usize),        // (clause_idx, sig_idx) of CallClosure
    MakeClosure(usize),              // stmt_idx of a MakeClosure stmt
}
type EmitterSite = (SpecKey, BlockId, EmitSlot);

// In WorklistState:
produces: HashMap<EmitterSite, SpecKey>          // what each site currently emits
holders : HashMap<SpecKey, HashSet<EmitterSite>> // who keeps each spec alive

// Auxiliary for MakeClosure / opaque dispatch:
opaque_arities           : HashSet<usize>                // grows monotone
pending_makeclosure_arity: HashMap<usize, HashSet<EmitterSite>>
\`\`\`

### Algorithm

When walking spec S:

  1. Compute a complete \`Vec<(EmitterSite, SpecKey)>\` for S's body.
  2. For each emitter site E in S:
     - Let K_new = the emitted target (None if E currently emits nothing,
       e.g., a MakeClosure waiting on an arity).
     - Let K_old = \`produces.get(&E)\`.
     - If K_new == K_old: no-op.
     - Else:
       - If K_old.is_some(): remove E from holders[K_old].
       - If K_new.is_some(): add E to holders[K_new], set produces[E] = K_new.
                              If K_new not in specs, enqueue it.
       - If K_old.is_some() and not equal: also leave \`produces[E]\` set
         to K_new (or remove if K_new.is_none()).

  3. Recompute S's effective_return (existing logic). If changed,
     enqueue every spec in return_readers[S].

When walking encounters an unresolved CallClosure/spawn of arity K:
  - \`if opaque_arities.insert(K) { ... drain pending_makeclosure_arity[K]
    ... enqueue each pending E's caller_spec for re-walk }\`

When walking encounters MakeClosure with opaque_arity K:
  - If K ∈ opaque_arities: emit (E, lambda_spec_key) normally.
  - Else: \`pending_makeclosure_arity.entry(K).or_default().insert(E)\`,
    emit nothing this round.

### End of typing

Reachability from \`entry_seeds\` via the holders graph:

\`\`\`rust
let mut reachable: HashSet<SpecKey> = entry_seeds(m).into_iter().collect();
let mut q: VecDeque<SpecKey> = reachable.iter().cloned().collect();
while let Some(spec) = q.pop_front() {
    // Walk forward through what this spec emits, NOT what its key would
    // look like under recomputation. produces gives us the actual edges.
    for E in /* emitter sites whose caller_spec == spec */ {
        if let Some(K) = produces.get(&E)
            && reachable.insert(K.clone()) {
            q.push_back(K);
        }
    }
}
specs.retain(|k, _| reachable.contains(k));
\`\`\`

This is the cycle collector for orphan cycles (mutual recursion
that lost its external anchor). Single BFS pass; no key
recomputation; cannot diverge from what the walker emitted.

## Why this is correct

- Every spec in \`specs\` has ≥1 entry in \`holders\` by construction.
  (Inserted when first emitted; removed when last emitter changes
  its target.)
- Cycles in the holders graph are handled exactly the same way
  they are in any tracing GC: reachable from a root = alive.
- The walker/sweep key-recomputation duplication that hid the
  closure_lit bug (PROV.1) cannot recur: there is no recomputation.

## Removals

- \`compute_reachable_specs\` function entirely (~125 lines in
  \`src/ir_typer.rs\`).
- \`sweep_makeclosure\` function entirely (~50 lines).
- The \`loop { sweep; drain; if no new break }\` block in
  \`type_module\` (~30 lines).
- The walker/sweep duplication of cont-key construction logic.
- The implicit assumption that "stale specs from old slot-0 reads
  get cleaned up at the end" — there are no stale specs anymore.

## Walkthrough hazards (and resolutions)

- **EmitSlot granularity for Call**: a Call terminator emits TWO
  targets (Direct callee + Cont). \`EmitSlot::Direct\` and
  \`EmitSlot::Cont\` are separate variants. Both share block_id.

- **EmitSlot granularity for CallClosure with closure_lit**: each
  (clause_idx, sig_idx) in the closure's Descr.funcs DNF is a
  distinct emitter. \`EmitSlot::ClosureLit(c, s)\`.

- **Diff cost per walk**: O(emitters_in_body) Vec<Descr> equality
  checks. TYP.1's structural fast-path makes each cheap when
  unchanged. Negligible overhead.

- **Eager delete vs end-of-typing reachability**: don't eager-delete
  when holders[K] empties — orphan cycles would survive (eval calls
  eval; if main stops emitting eval, eval.holders = {self}, refcount
  > 0). End-of-typing reachability handles this in one BFS pass.

- **return_readers stays**: separate concern from holders. Tracks
  who reads whose return for effective_returns propagation. Not
  affected by this refactor.

## Acceptance

- All fixture matrix tests pass (interp + JIT + AOT) with NO further
  golden re-blessing beyond what fz-5j5 already did.
- PROV.4's counter bounds stay green (or tighten in the same commit
  if they improve).
- \`compute_reachable_specs\` is gone. \`sweep_makeclosure\` is gone.
- No reference in \`ir_typer.rs\` to "stale specs" or "over-
  specialization" — the invariant rules it out.

## Independent of

PROV.1, .2, .3, .5. Should land after PROV.4 (perf pin) so the
counter test catches any regression. PROV.7 follows.
title: fz-PROV.6: provenance-based spec lifetime — EmitterSite + holders + unified worklist
